밭의 정성과 기다림이 어긋나 작물이 시들어 버렸습니다. 그러나 아버지가: Your care and your patience fell out of step, and the crop withered away. Even so, you learn what Father battled every dawn.
The field is calm. It would be fine to wait a while.
The work went on, but the bee kept tugging at my mind.
배드엔딩: 아직은 쓰기만 한 맛: Bad Ending: A Taste Still Only Bitter
After the bee flew off, a warm wind drifted over the field.
The bee startled and lifted off a few times, but in the end it turned toward the flower bed.
Tap the leaves in order for the bee to rest on, and lead it to the flower bed.
Grew a rice plant and reaped its rice.
벼를 마우스 왼쪽 버튼으로 누른 채: Hold down the left mouse button on the rice plant
It can spread disease. Clear it away.
To think you have to tend even the places you can't see.
Caring for the places no one sees — that was the real touch of a farmer's hand.
The warmth of a hand tending what no one ever sees.
The mounding was left half-done — the roots still feel bare.
Left half-mounded — the roots feel bare.
The mounded soil swells soft and full.
It was a thing to be done before the rain ever arrived.
It has to be done before the rain comes.
You'd think rain means rest, but the drainage has to be cleared while it's still falling.
비닐봉지가 문고리에 부딪히는 소리가 난다.\n아버지는 흙 묻은 당근을 꺼:
A plastic bag rustles against the door handle.
Father pulls out a soil-covered carrot and says, 'Today's are sweet.'

A plastic bag knocks against the door handle.
Father pulls out a soil-dusted {crop} and says, 'This one's sweet today.'
{name}, instead of answering, only fidgeted with a cup of water.
Here was a heart that reckoned with the rain before it came.
Revealed once you accomplish a secret farmstead feat.
Building back up, with dry bare palms, a furrow that had slumped under wind and rain, mounding it firm again.
Rain drives the moisture up sharply — watch for overwatering.
Spilled a lot of the rain.
Pull them and pull them, and still they come back.
There were no weeds to pull — I only churned up the poor soil for nothing.
The snap of a root lingers on my fingertips.
It blocks the roots' path. Clear it away.
The roots hold on deeper than I thought.
Raised an apple tree to fruit and picked an apple.
A closer look — no serious pests.
Damp earth clings heavy to the tip of the spade.
Tape is wound around the shovel's handle. So it won't hurt my hands.
The new hoe cuts well. And yet, somehow, it feels like a stranger's.
새 호미를 꺼내 쓴다: Take out a new hoe and use it
새벽 4시.\n아직 어두운 하늘 아래, 아버지는 장화를 신었다.\n오늘도:
Four in the morning.
Under a sky still dark, Father pulled on his boots.
The boy won't eat his carrots today either.
Still, they have to be watered.
Because this soil is waiting.
You were clumsy and made mistakes, but each time you reached for the soil again.

The feel of soil on my fingertips is becoming familiar.

My palms are stinging. How many times did Father do this?
My hands are growing rough. I think of the backs of Father's hands.
My hands ache. And he did this every single day.
The mended hoe never left my hand the whole day's work.
Your hands were skilled, but reading the heart is still ahead of you.
Found every water vein! The water flows through.

The carrot he harvested was too small, marked all over by haste.
How to wait, and what his father faced alone every dawn — none of it has reached him yet.
Newly awake,

The harvested {crop} was too small, marked with haste.
How to wait, and what Father faced alone every dawn — none of it has reached you yet.
Waking from the dream, {name} passes the table without a word, thinking:
'Still bitter. I think I need to stand here a while longer.'
수확한 당근을 베어 문 순간, 다정한 침묵이 밭을 감싼다.\n모든 것을:
The moment he bites into the carrot he harvested, a tender silence settles over the field.
He doesn't understand it all yet, but the worth of every drop of his father's sweat quietly rises within him.
Newly awake,

The moment you bite into the harvested {crop}, a tender silence wraps the {field}.
You don't understand all of it, but the worth of every drop of Father's sweat quietly rises in your heart.
Waking from the dream, {name} gazes at the {food} on the table, then slowly chews and swallows.
'I think I understand, a little. That silence of yours, Dad.'
수확한 당근을 베어 문 순간, 세상이 황금빛으로 물든다.\n아버지의 땀과:
The moment he bites into the carrot he harvested, the world is washed in golden light.
A sweetness that holds his father's sweat and all his waiting.
Newly awake,

The moment you bite into the harvested {crop}, the world is steeped in gold.
A sweetness that holds Father's sweat and his long waiting.
Waking from the dream, {name} sits down first at the table and picks up the {food} to eat.
'Dad, I'll come out with you at dawn tomorrow. Teach me everything.'
As I catch my breath, the whole field comes into view.
It took time, but it stands firm now. The more you hurry, the longer the road — I understand that saying now.
시듦엔딩: 끝내 지켜내지 못한 밭: Withered Ending: The Field You Couldn't Save
식탁 위에 당근 반찬이 놓여 있다.\n: A carrot dish sits on the table.
식탁 위에 당근 반찬이 놓여 있다.\n{name_eun} 젓가락으로 그릇:
The {food} sits on the table, set out as a side dish.
{name} pushed the bowl aside with {utensil}, and Father fell quiet for a moment.
Back then, I didn't understand why that silence felt so long.
식탁 위에 여태 남겨두었던 마지막 당근 한 조각까지 모두 삼켜 냈다.\n:
You swallowed down even the last bite of {food} you'd always left on the table.
In that instant, the heavy guilt pressing on your throat vanished like a lie.
Beyond the cracking red sky, in the morning you finally met, the table was wiped clean.
'Leave nothing behind. This time, all the way.' Those words settled quietly in your heart.
식탁 위의 당근 반찬이 떠오른다.\n이번에는 밀어내고 싶다는 생각보다,:
The {food} on the table comes to mind.
This time, before the urge to push it away, comes the wish to really taste it, just once.
Harvested flawlessly, without a single mistake.
I kept making mistakes. There's still so much I don't know.
싱크대 옆에 남은 반찬 그릇이 보인다.\n작은 주황색 조각들이 물에 젖어:
A leftover side-dish bowl sits by the sink.
Little orange pieces, soaked through, slowly sink in the water.
Somehow the scent of soil in the dream grows thicker.
쌀밥을 떠 먹는다: spoons up the rice and eats it
쌀밥을 입에 넣은: put the rice in their mouth
Telling what to keep from what to throw away always takes so many hands.
Throw the seeds away too, and there'll be nothing left to harvest.
Move the seeds to the field on the left, the debris to the bin on the right.
아래 '농장 일지'는 밭의 증상만 알려줄 뿐, 무엇을 할지는 직접 판단해: The 'Farm Journal' below only tells you the field's symptoms — deciding what to do is up to you. When you're at a loss, 'Observe' gives you a close diagnosis and the weather forecast.
Is it all right to do nothing at all?
It was care given where no one could see.
아무리 다독여도 당근은 다시 일어서지 못했다.\n흙만 남은 두둑을 오래:
No matter how you tended it, the {crop} never rose again.
You stared a long while at the ridge where only bare soil remained.
Even so, those countless dawns were not in vain.
What Father struggled against every day — now you understand it, a little.
Father laughs and brushes the soil from the back of his hand.
The sight is tender, more than heavy.
Even the {field} in the dream feels a little less strange now.
아버지는 아직 어두운 시간에 밭으로 나갔다.\n흙을 털어내는 손등에는 잔:
Father went out to the {field} while it was still dark.
The back of his hand, brushing off the soil, was crossed with fine lines.
The thought that those hands made the food on the table comes to me, late.
Did Father water them at this same early hour?
Father must have waited like this too — every day.
아버지의 땀과 오랜 기다림의 깊은 의미를 완벽히 이해하여, 식탁에서 남기: Having fully understood the deep meaning of Father's sweat and long patience, you finish the carrots you once left on the table and head out to the dawn field beside him.
아버지의 말이 떠오른다. '물이 많다고 좋은 게 아니란다.': Father's words come back to me. 'More water isn't always better.'
Father's field, where it all begins. A balanced, beginner's crop.
I think I finally understand why Father's hands were so rough.
아버지의 시간을 깊이 들여다보세요: and look deeper into Father's time
A whole day of Father's had become a single meal of mine.
Father's days must have been filled with a quiet like this.
Because it's a carrot my child will eat.
Will my child ever touch this soil, someday?
My child won't know it, but this too is love.
아직 겪지 않은 일: An event you haven't lived yet
Not grown enough yet. Rush it and you'll ruin everything.
This ending hasn't been unlocked yet.
You haven't yet closed the distance to that heart.

Held out to the end of Night)mareFarm and harvested.

Drag it to the right place.

Somewhere, a low voice rings out.
'What you left behind, you must eat it all, here.
And to eat it, you'll have to grow it again.'

Only by raising a crop to the very end and harvesting it in this dark-red field
can you wake from the dream.
여느 밤과 같은 꿈인 줄 알았다.\n\n그런데 하늘이 검붉다. 흙에서 탄:
I thought it was a dream like any other night.

But the sky is a deep, dark red. The soil reeks of smoke.
In the middle of the field, familiar plates lie stacked —
all the food I've left uneaten, until now.

Somewhere, a low voice rings out.
'What you left behind, you must eat it all, here.
And to eat it, you'll have to grow it again.'

Only by raising a crop to the very end and harvesting it in this dark-red field
can you wake from the dream.
It takes several tries before it pops free — it won't come in one go.
I gave it too much water today.
He'll leave it again today, I know. Even so.
오늘은 비가 올 것 같다.\n아버지는 배수로를 미리 정리하고,\n아이가:
It looks like rain today.
Father clears the drainage ahead of time,
and sets a pair of gloves for the boy beside the field.
Just in case.
The boy won't notice, but that's all right.
He never did it to be noticed.

The bee I carried over found the flowers, and small fruit set along the field's edge.
Our field is safe, but I worry for the neighbor's.
The night you sealed the gap in the fence for good, so the deer couldn't slip through and tear up the field.
울타리 틈으로 고라니 한 마리가 서성인다.\n어린 잎을 노리는 눈치다.\:
A deer paces at a gap in the fence.
It has its eye on the young leaves.
Chase it off, and it slips back through any opening.

울타리 틈을 나뭇가지로 막는다: Plug the gap in the fence with branches
Leave food on your plate, and you'll have to eat every bit of it in hell. You begin in a dark-crimson field.
This sound of running water is like the scent Father carried when he worked.
This field, at least, I'll keep clean.
The furrows in this field are unusually straight. The work of a long-practiced hand.
This field is the whole of my day.
This is the hardest stretch of all to bear.
I can feel the weight of this work.
There were hands that kept this place, every single day.
Beneath this soil, a long patience lies buried.
Into this soil, my hours quietly gather.
이 흙에 왜 물을 줘야 하는 거지?: Why does this soil even need watering?
In this soil, someone's hours lie layered, one upon another.
Keeping this soil from drying out — every day, just like this.
Is there even an end to this?
Leave this in the field and the carrot will struggle to grow.
I still don't understand why I have to do this.
So this, too, was part of farming.
I don't see what difference this makes.
At this rate, the field is lost. One more wilting and there'll be nothing left to save.
이런 걸 맨손으로?: You do this with bare hands?
이번 꿈에서 남은 기록: The Record Left by This Dream
The day you held back the fierce water that came surging in without warning from the neighbor's field.
이웃 밭에서 물이 넘쳐 흘러오고 있다.\n우리 밭도 위험할 수 있다.\n:
Water is spilling over from the next field.
Ours could be in danger too.
What should I do?
In the end, a few young sprouts in the neighbor's field were lost to the water.

Something's stirring on the undersides of the leaves.
I'd never once looked at the underside of a leaf.
잎 뒤로 벌레가 보인다: Bugs are showing on the undersides of the leaves
Caught nearly all the bugs under the leaves.
Picked the bugs off the undersides of the leaves.

I can't leave even a single leaf to chance.
So this is what it means to turn over every last leaf.
The underside of a leaf is softer than I'd have guessed.

Crops grow from proper care — and from 'Wait,' when the field is at peace. Haste only harms the carrot.  (M key: Mute)
Grew a crop all the way through for the very first time.
Grew and reaped crops a hundred times in all.
Grew and reaped crops ten times in all.
Even the smallest thing can't just be passed over.
Tiny legs creep along the veins of a leaf.
Well cleared. The soil is much cleaner now.

젓가락 끝에 걸린 당근 한 조각이 이상하게 무겁다.\n맛보다 먼저 떠오르:
A single piece of {food} caught on the {utensil} feels strangely heavy.
Before its taste, what rose first was someone's waiting.
정답은 알려주지 않습니다: No answers are handed to you
So this is what devotion really means.
The wet earth smells like the air after rain.
A touch short — the soil is still dry.
Little by little, I think I'm starting to understand.
It was a day when you felt you were beginning to understand.
Reining in your impatience is part of the work too.
I mustn't rush it. Not the carrot, and not the child.
You wait, quietly. The carrot grows at its own pace.
To watch over it quietly — so this was love, day after day.
Good. The seed found its place.
좌우로 번갈아 흔들어 캐내세요!: Rock it side to side to work it loose!
Rock it side to side, bit by bit, to work it out.
좌우로 흔들어 이삭을 털고 (탈곡),: Shake side to side to thresh the grain heads (threshing),
Shake side to side to thresh the grain heads, then rub to hull them.
The one who prepares is always the one no one sees.
지금 흙을 퍼 올려 다시 쌓는다: Scoop the soil up now and rebuild it
This isn't the time to tend the drainage.
지켜내지 못한 논: The paddy I failed to protect
지켜내지 못한 밭: The field I failed to protect
Guarding something is always quiet, and always the same work over and over.
Sealing the way came before chasing it off. Father would have done it that way.
Cold water wets the back of my hand.
Something warm rises up through the cold soil.
창고 구석에서 손잡이가 반질반질한 호미를 찾았다.\n날은 녹슬었지만, 손:
In a corner of the shed I found a hoe, its handle worn smooth.
The blade is rusted, but it settles into the hand at a strangely easy angle.
The angle of a thing used for years.
창고 먼지 쌓인 구석에서 발견해 녹을 문질러 길들였던 손때 묻은 아버지의: Father's hoe, worn smooth by his hands — found in a dusty corner of the shed, its rust rubbed away and coaxed back to life.

The smell of green leaves soaks into my hands.
How many times a day did he do this?
But soon you will wake — beside the one you love.
One spot came out patchy, but the big gap was gone.
After one handful pulled, the furrow looks so much wider.
Turning each leaf, one by one, my hands grow calm.
한 조각의 당근을 입에 넣으며 아버지를 쳐다봅니다. 모든 걸 알진 못해도: You slip a single piece of carrot into your mouth and look up at Father. You don't understand it all, but a tender silence drifts across the field.
한 조각의 무게: The Weight of a Single Bite
한 조각의 시간: The Time Within a Single Slice
Blocking it together, the water settled all the better. I wasn't alone.
Thanks to the channel we closed together, the neighbor's field weathered the rains too.
The sun slips lower, little by little.
Gathered plenty of sunlight! The field is warm.

The scarecrow is up, but so hastily it looks ready to topple again any moment.

times

On cloudy days the soil dries slowly.
흙 묻은 당근: A carrot still dusted with soil
There seems to be the faintest sound down in the soil.
A pair of gloves lies on the soil. My size.
So even the soil needs tending by hand.
The soil was cold, but the seed was warm.
As my hand pats down the soil, Father's days settle over mine.
Patting the soil down turns out to be such a quiet kind of work.
As you pat the soil down, life stirs back into it.
Mounded up generously, the soil brings a wave of life back to it.
Worked the soil, but nothing much changed.
The hands working the soil are, before I know it, growing to look like Father's.
Worked the soil a fair amount.
흙을 왜 또 만져야 하지?: Why do I have to work the soil again?
Even drinking in the water takes the soil its own time.
The soil is bone-dry, crumbling against my fingertips.
Soil works its way under my nails.
The soil is sodden, and water is pooling on top.
흩어진 별을 이어, 아버지가 보던 새벽 하늘을 그렸다.\n\n이은 별::
By joining the scattered stars, I traced out the dawn sky Father used to watch.

Stars connected:
